question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here are all the hook events a Juju charm can observe via Ops:

## Lifecycle Hooks
- **install** — runs once when the unit is first deployed
- **config-changed** — runs when configuration changes (and after install, upgrade, and leader-elected)
- **start** — runs after install and config-changed on first deployment
- **stop** — runs when the unit is about to be destroyed
- **remove** — runs when the unit is being removed
- **upgrade-charm** — runs when the charm code is upgraded

## Leader Hooks
- **leader-elected** — runs on the unit that becomes leader
- **leader-settings-changed** — runs on non-leader units when leader settings change

## Relation Hooks
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-created** — a new relation is established
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-joined** — a new unit joins the relation
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-changed** — relation data changes
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-departed** — a unit leaves the relation
- **\<relation-name\>-relation-broken** — the relation is fully removed

## Storage Hooks
- **\<storage-name\>-storage-attached** — storage is attached to the unit
- **\<storage-name\>-storage-detaching** — storage is about to be detached

## Container Hooks (Kubernetes charms)
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-ready** — Pebble is ready in a sidecar container
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-custom-notice** — a custom Pebble notice fires
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-check-failed** — a Pebble health check fails
- **\<container-name\>-pebble-check-recovered** — a Pebble health check recovers

## Action Hooks
- **\<action-name\>-action** — an action is invoked by the user

## Secret Hooks
- **secret-changed** — a secret the charm tracks has a new revision
- **secret-rotate** — a secret owned by the charm needs rotation
- **secret-remove** — an old secret revision can be removed
- **secret-expired** — a secret revision has expired

## Other
- **collect-unit-status** — called before status is reported (unit level)
- **collect-app-status** — called before status is reported (app level, leader only)
- **update-status** — runs periodically (every ~5 minutes by default)
- **pre-series-upgrade** / **post-series-upgrade** — for OS series upgrades (machine charms, legacy)

In Ops, these are observed as Python events (e.g. `self.on.install`, `self.on.config_changed`, `self.on.<relation>_relation_joined`, etc.).
